questions.text: If α and β are the zeroes of the quadratic polynomial f(x) = ax² + bx + c, then α + β is equal to:
questions.explanation: For a quadratic polynomial, the sum of the zeroes is given by the formula -b/a (coefficient of x divided by coefficient of x²).

questions.text: A quadratic polynomial whose zeroes are -3 and 4 is:
questions.explanation: Polynomial = x² - (sum of zeroes)x + (product). Sum = -3+4=1; Product = -3*4=-12. Thus, x² - x - 12.

questions.text: The number of zeroes a cubic polynomial can have is at most:
questions.explanation: The maximum number of zeroes a polynomial can have is equal to its degree. A cubic polynomial has degree 3.

questions.text: If one zero of the quadratic polynomial x² + 3x + k is 2, then the value of k is:
The zeroes of the quadratic polynomial x² + 99x + 127 are:
questions.explanation: Since all coefficients are positive, the sum ( -b/a) is negative and product (c/a) is positive. This only happens if both zeroes are negative.
